title: Level 25
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:> Level 25: "Out of Order"}}{{User:TheBackroomsMapMaker/25/Important/25 Theme}}
{{Audio|align=center|file=Mega Man X5 OST- Unused Track "Deep Sea".mp3|volume=2|loop=true|secrettext=Mega Man X5 OST- Unused Track "Deep Sea"}} What you could hear in '''Level 25'''.
[[File:Level25-189.png|center|400x400px|thumb|link=]]
Here is '''Level 25'''. This is an image I took a long time ago that I just found while looking under the arcade machines. '''Level 25''' is the '''26th''' level of '''[[The Backrooms]]'''. '''Level 25''' seems to represent an abandoned and dusty video game arcade, filled with arcade machines, if that wasn't obvious enough. Every arcade game requires a quarter to start. If you ever run out of quarters or don't have any, then this level is useless for you. I first found '''Level 25''' after playing an arcade machine in '''[[Level 27]]'''. Back then, '''Level 25''' didn't have a level number. I called it '''Level 25''' because a quarter is worth 25 cents, and all the arcade machines need a quarter to work. '''Level 25''' is like a party; it's filled with pizzas and '''[[Object 1|beverages]]'''! Just saying, the beverages taste a '''[[Object 12|little weird]]'''.. The room I'm in right now is filled with classic games like pool tables, ice hockey, whack the mole, chess, and 4 in a row! I can see some weird hallways with hotel rooms. The hallways have rooms '''1061-1070, 1071-1073, and 1081-1099'''. Rooms '''1074-1080''' seem to be '''[[Phenomenon 14|missing]]'''. '''But ignore that, I'd like to stay here forever and ever!'''
[[File:level25.png|center|400x400px|thumb|link=|
'''''The oldest known image taken within Level 25; the photograph was captured c. 1984.'''''
]]
{{User:TheBackroomsMapMaker/Headerbox Border|file=Level-40-cc.jpg|speed=50|border=blue|header=
Sorry, this machine is out of order. Don't hesitate to get in touch with one of our employees for assistance.
}}
{{Image Banner|image=Level-40-cc.jpg|caption=Welcome to '''Level 25'''! Enjoy the arcade machines, as this is a safe level.}}
{{Alt Class|ent=1|no=25|ext=2|env=0}}
'''Level 25''' is known as the '''26th''' level currently known within '''[[The Backrooms]]'''. It is assumed to have been discovered on 12/07/1981, found by a series of journals about '''Level 25''' left by a soon-to-be-disclosed wanderer.
{{SimpleHeader|header=Overview_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}}
{{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil|file=Arcade255.jpg|𝚏𝚒𝚐=1.0|hovertext=At least the smallest sign of life can be seen in this echo of an arcade.|caption=
At least the smallest sign of life can be seen in this echo of an arcade.
}} '''Level 25''', also known as '''"Out of Order"''' or '''"The Arcade,"''' is a unique and intriguing level that recalls nostalgia for the mid-to-late 1980s arcade era. '''Level 25''' is presented as a series of interconnected, segmented rooms, decorated with a variety of mid-to-late 80s arcade cabinets that fill the majority of the rooms. Some are out of order or have been destroyed beyond recognition, providing an electrical hazard at the level. The rooms are largely or fully built of echoey, crumbling concrete, with carpeting on the floor. The designs and decorations match the level's theme. While '''Level 25''' has yet to be fully explored and documented, most likely due to a slightly hazardous or congested environment, it is one of the smallest levels in '''[[The Backrooms]]''', spanning exactly 2,961 miles. At first sight, the level's theme seems to fit in with the aforementioned antique arcade ambiance, with the composition being an outer space theme. {{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil|file=40wikidot.png|𝚏𝚒𝚐=2.0|hovertext=At least the smallest sign of life can be seen in this echo of an arcade.|caption=
An image of the interstellar emptiness, representing [[Level 40|an old level]]..
}} '''Level 25''' distinguishes itself from other levels by its decorations and aesthetics, which, along with the level's abandonment to nature, generally range from decorations such as bright yet dim neon lights imitating stars to hand-made solar systems hanging from the ceiling, proving to be a highly admirable sight. '''Level 25''' sometimes gives a feeling of broken memories, like an arcade you used to visit as a child, collapsed into emotional decay. The level feels depressing and dull, sometimes having very low saturated colors. At least the smallest sign of life can be seen in this echo of an arcade. The level can make you remember bad memories of your life, stuff that you wanted to forget about. It makes you feel very dull and lonely, fitting the level itself. Another notable feature of the level is the carpet, which is either a dark neon-colored carpet with occasional visual representations of planets and stars or a pitch-black rug with randomly distributed white dots indicating stars. This carpet's design motif is not restricted to the floor; it can also be used on the walls and ceiling. This outer space theme looks to include aspects that can emit light in areas lacking light sources, particularly in darkness. {{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil|file=Level25-1.png|𝚏𝚒𝚐=3.0|hovertext=A forgotten childhood.|caption=
A forgotten childhood.
}} The LED lights in the ceiling are often dim. Nonetheless, they are not the level's major light sources because several rooms lack ceiling lights. Instead, coin-operated machines and operable arcade cabinets provide the primary lighting. In the absence of arcade equipment, the carpet might lighten, keeping the area from becoming too dark. However, if none of the above are present, the region will be totally dark. Air conditioning is installed throughout the level to keep it cool and comfortable. Aside from arcade machines, several coin-operated machine games do not require coins and can be played for free in huge rooms. Arcade cabinets and devices are constantly turned on and hooked into power outlets. Some may still not plug in, rendering them unusable, and this is common for all machines in the room. The arcade appliance may not be operable because certain rooms lack or have restricted power outlets. {{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil|file=Outoforder.jpg|𝚏𝚒𝚐=4.0|hovertext=The start of memory lane.|caption=
The start of memory lane.
}} The level's ambiance is silent and secluded, since all gaming machines produce no sound unless wanderers engage with and play them, in which case the game's sound effects become audible. In certain regions, the calm tones of vintage 80s pop music become more intense, yet this song always makes travelers nostalgic for their childhoods. Aside from such game devices, there are technologies, such as the Nintendo GameBoy, NES, and others, but they are all from the 1980s and lack WiFi connectivity. There are vintage Coke and snack vending machines, and each vending machine may include items for survival. Paper "Out of Order" signs can be placed on vending machines to signal that they are not in operation and cannot accept items, while they are impossible to damage. The interior of these vending machines usually has a large amount of items and supplies; expensive items are present, but the machines malfunction. These vending machines believe they are fraudulent and cause internal delusions. {{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil|file=GenerationRetro.png|𝚏𝚒𝚐=5.0|hovertext=Retro and lonely arcades, like a faded memory.|caption=
Retro and lonely arcades, like a faded memory.
}} Not all places are arcades; some may be indoor playgrounds, vast eating spaces, or bowling lanes. Indoor playgrounds are made up of a variety of indoor play spaces that are joined together to form a single structure, or they might be trampoline parks. The eating facilities are large rooms with plenty of chairs and tables, as well as surrounding eateries. Fast food and 1980s-style pizza are still commonly accessible, free, and regularly replenished in most venues. These areas feature a lot of vending machines, and the smell of fast food, pizza, and flat Coke permeates the entire area. The bowling lanes are typical neon bowling alleys, sharing many of the arcades' features. Although the safety of some foods and beverages is called into doubt due to those found on the table and floor in various rooms, they are generally ruined, having expired a long time ago, and are unconsumable, except for those that may still be ingested and drunk safely—depending on the type. Regardless of their expiration dates, everything served in vending machines is safe to eat and drink. It appears to keep food and beverages from expiring. Doors in the arcade section go to the bathrooms rather than another arcade area. Doors with "Employees Only" signage lead to the back unoccupied areas. They may hold containers or older, long, un-steamed, and chilly boilers. The latter frequently contains common products such as almond water or other snack-based foods. Many abandoned arcade games remain playable. However, the majority of them are shattered or somehow damaged. Multiple TV stations are set up around '''Level 25''', which contain many commercials for various items that seem to be random and unbelievable. Many advertisements were recorded, but most were either completely unintelligible or corrupted beyond repair. Many other advertisements are not recorded and can be seen in '''Level 25''', and various other levels.
{{SimpleHeader|header=Arcade Cabinets_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}}
{{Blurimage Border|text-color=white|border-color=white|box-shadow-color=nil|file=Quarterhub.webp|𝚏𝚒𝚐=6.0|hovertext=Fragmented nostalgia.|caption=
Fragmented nostalgia.
}} The rest of the level is occupied and decorated with video game arcade machines. They are available in a variety of styles and sizes, just as they were in the early to mid-1980s. However, they are all operational and, for the most part, compatible with games from that era. Some arcade games are well-known in [[The Frontrooms]], while others are absent, and some appear to have no titles and arbitrary aims. These inactive or battered video game cabinets can be plundered, scavenged, bent, or smashed using a variety of means. These cabinets might clutter abandoned areas or workers' quarters. However, only a small fraction of arcade cabinets—roughly one in every 1500—have unusual features. These machine cabinets were retrofitted with the ability to transport their current users to a predetermined level in [[The Backrooms]]. These devices initially show nothing except a dark screen. When a user interacts with the gadget, a pixelated image of the level they will advance to shows. By hitting any button on the machine cabinet again, a person can teleport to the next level of The Backrooms, which location matches to the image on the screen. For whatever reason, these arcade cabinets are different from standard ones, which can be destroyed. If they do, the arcade cabinets will no longer be able to function as exits. These incidents have occurred for [[M.E.G.]] when arcade cabinets that transfer to [[Level 10]] have been completely non-functional owing to water damage. As of current, all found anomalous arcade cabinets can transport their users to the following levels: [[Level 0]], [[Level 3]], [[Level 5]], [[Level 6]], [[Level 9]], [[Level 10]], [[Level 11]], [[Level 12]], [[Level 19]], [[Level 21]], [[Level 24]], [[Level 26]], [[Level 29]], [[Level 37]], [[Level 41]], [[Level 50]], [[Level 87]], [[Level 89]], [[Level 90]], or [[The End]]. It should be noted that entering levels such as [[Level 87]] or [[The End]] won't spawn an arcade cabinet to exit.
{{SimpleHeader|header=Other Areas_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}}
{{SimpleHeader|header=Pizza Places_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}} One may find various pizza places in the area, which is a Faceling-run pizza restaurant in various locations within the arcade. This area sells a variety of pizzas and beverages, priding itself on being one of the only establishments to sell imitations of Earthly food brands. If a wanderer makes a commotion, they will be led away from the pizza place. Fast food and 1980s-style pizza are still commonly accessible, free, and regularly replenished in most venues. These areas feature a lot of vending machines, and the smell of fast food, pizza, and flat Coke permeates the entire area. These foods range from being free to costing several bottles of [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. The chairs, tables, floors, walls, and ceilings of the pizza place are made of concrete decorated with arcade-themed artwork, adorned with bright neon lights. TVs from the 90s and above can also be found attached to the walls. Although they function quite similarly to TVs in [[The Frontrooms]], the channels that they offer are known to change every so often, ranging from backrooms-related news to entertainment videos. {{SimpleHeader|header=Bowling Alleys_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}} Bowling Lanes may be found near pizza places, which are typical neon bowling alleys, sharing many of the arcades' features, with them ranging in quality and structure; some bowling lanes are in near-perfect condition, while others are severely damaged and abandoned. The bowling arcades are decorated with bright neon lights, various patterns and colors, with each one being unique and different from others. Various bowling pins are scattered throughout the bowling alleys along with bowling balls that each vary in different sizes and colors, which show minimal damage. Various chairs and tables with different sizes and colors populate the area, with various objects, especially food. Although the safety of some foods and beverages is called into doubt due to those found on the table and floor in various rooms, they are generally ruined, having expired a long time ago, and are unconsumable, except for those that may still be ingested and drunk safely depending on the type. Regardless of their expiration dates, everything served in vending machines is safe to eat and drink. It appears to keep food and beverages from expiring. {{SimpleHeader|header=The Warehouse_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}} '''Level 25''' provides an entrance to '''The Warehouse'''. Usually a metal door or a stairwell concealed behind storage rooms or under ramps serves as the entry. '''The Warehouse''' is an underground, cramped basement, poorly lit, and heavily dependent on artificial fluorescent lighting, in contrast to the spacious, open arcade above. '''The Warehouse''' has no definite area; however, they cover a majority of the underground section within '''Level 25''' and includes several floors. '''The Warehouse''' is arranged like a labyrinth of little rooms, storage spaces, and passages, which serves as a storage section of '''Level 25'''. The walls are mostly made of unfinished concrete, with damp areas, peeling gray paint, and occasionally exposed pipes. Because of the low ceilings, wanderers frequently have to crouch in some areas of the level. Vending machines, lockers, boxes, damaged equipment and most commonly arcade machines are strewn throughout the section, and minor objects may also be found, such as nails, cardboard boxes and torn papers may be found more commonly throughout the section.
{{SimpleHeader|header=Addendum_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}}
You may see now why I named '''Level 25''' "The Arcade Hub," since the arcade cabinets can lead to many levels of the backrooms and possibly every level. This level is unlike [[Level 21]], which will take you to a random level, but the place where every arcade cabinet may take you to a specific level. By simply finding the arcades that look similar to each other, when you interact by pressing a button, it will activate and show a picture of the level that it will lead you to, and if you interact again, it will take you by noclip to that level. I think the cabinet's color might indicate the level that will go on, but I am not sure. This level connects to many, many others. It acts as a hub of sorts. For what or why? I don't know, but I feel that someone most likely did. Like in the past, there might have been a group or organization here once, and they tried to make these arcade cabinets connect to various levels of the Backrooms, so they used this level as their method of travel to find supplies or trade with bases in that level. However, all of this is just my theory. Many people misunderstood this level; they thought it was a trap with no way out that would entice people to keep playing arcade games indefinitely, making it seem like a false version of heaven. I'm not sure why people were thinking that way. I recall an occurrence similar to the one that affected [[Level 130|Level 13]], which caused all level exits to lock, making it impossible for wanderers to leave. However, because the entries were still functional, any new wanderers who entered were also stuck there. It happened for about six months, and '''Level 25''' became missing from the database. After the phenomenon passed, exits became functional, yet people remembered '''Level 25''' unfavorably.
— [[User:KingSheep17|KingSheep17]], the one who aforementionedly discovered '''Level 25'''.
'''Level 25''' is nearly fully devoid of any life. The only entities found are [[Kats]], typically observed taking a nap inside broken arcade cabinets or exploring and interacting with their surroundings, and [[Entity 9|Facelings]], viewed "interacting" with the arcade machines or serving as background commotion. Arcade Machines don't count as entities themselves, but instead classify as objects.
{{SimpleHeader|header=Bases, Outposts, and Communities_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}} There are no known bases, communities, or outposts in '''Level 25'''. {{SimpleHeader|header=M.E.G. Arcade Outpost_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}} *An outpost built by M.E.G. to gather arcade machines that can transfer to many levels so that they can explore and trade with outposts in other levels. *It consists of about twenty persons trying to gather the different materials. {{SimpleHeader|header=Entrances and Exits_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}} {{SimpleHeader|header=Entrances_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}} *Main entrance: Miniature wormholes in [[Level 24]] may appear on random intervals, though rare to encounter, entering one of them will take one to [[Level 25]]. Do not enter large worm holes or black holes, as it will rip wanderers apart. *Finding the arcade machine and interacting with it at a variety of levels may lead to '''Level 25'''. *'''Level 25''' is known to be accessed by entering a room, slightly resembling a janitor closet in [[Level 4]]. Simply staying in the room for enough time will cause one to fall unconscious and enter the arcade. *Finding a room full of paintings of an arcade in [[Level 29]] will also lead here. *It has been reported that if one manages to exit [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|The Party Rooms]] they may enter into '''Level 25.''' {{SimpleHeader|header=Exits_|background=rgba(125, 125, 125, 0.2)}} For exits, however, working arcade machines have been known to transport to the following levels: ---- {{Collapse|num=26|initial=> OPEN ARCADE EXITS|final=< CLOSE ARCADE EXITS|body=* [[Level 0]] * [[Level 5]] * [[Level 9]] * [[Level 10]] * [[Level 11]] * [[Level 19]] * [[Level 24]] * [[Level 29]] * [[Level 37]] * [[Level 50]] * [[Level 90]] * [[Level 108]] * [[Level 120]] * [[Level 141]] * [[Level 150]] * [[Level 165]] * [[Level 188]] * [[Level 201]] * [[Level 431]] * [[Level 790]] * [[Level 888]]}} *Interacting with certain arcade machines may lead to a vareity of levels, listed before. *Reaching the end of the level and going through the revolving door will land one in either [[Level 9]], [[Level 10]], or [[Level 11]]. *By chance, one will randomly be teleported to [[Level 26]]. *Noclipping into an arcade machine that appears to be old and dilapidated will send one to [[Level 40]]. Cabinets of specific colors will send one to the designated levels: *Green cabinets are the rarest; they lead to [[Level 24]]. *Blue cabinets are common, which lead to [[Level 6]]. *Orange cabinets lead to [[Level 11]]. *Yellow cabinets lead to [[Level 9]]. *Purple cabinets lead to [[Level 3]]. *Red cabinets lead to any anomalous level, like [[The Hub]]. *Golden cabinets lead to [[The End]]. *Gray cabinets lead to [[Level 1.5]]. *Black cabinets will kill the wanderer. *Going into any trash can will lead one to [[The Limitless Mulch]]. *White cabinets lead to [[Level 12]]. *Door with inscription 'backyard' lead to [[level 903]]
